body:
## Summary

Fixes #467.

This PR adds live terminal log watching for Telegram bot sessions on the current `main` codebase.

- Adds `/terminal_watch <uuid>` and reply-based `/terminal_watch` for `$ --status` isolation sessions.
- Updates a separate Telegram message with the latest terminal-sized log snapshot, then freezes it and attaches the full log when the session reaches a terminal status.
- Supports `--size WIDTHxHEIGHT`, `--width`, `--height`, `--interval-ms`, and `--max-chars` options.
- Keeps `--auto-start-screen-watch-message` experimental and off by default; auto-start only creates watch messages for public `/solve` sessions and skips private or unknown-visibility repositories.
- Reuses `/log` privacy behavior for manual watches: public logs can update in chat, private/unknown visibility is delivered by DM.
- Extracts `safeReply` from `telegram-bot.mjs` so the bot file remains below the 1500-line CI limit.
- Merges the branch with current `main` to resolve the previous dirty merge state.

## How to Reproduce

1. Start the bot with default screen isolation.
2. Run `/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123` in an authorized group.
3. Use the session UUID from the bot's execution message:
   - `/terminal_watch <uuid>`
   - or reply to the session message with `/terminal_watch --size 120x25`
4. Optional auto-start path: start the bot with `--auto-start-screen-watch-message` and run `/solve` against a public repository.

## Validation

- `npm run lint`
- `npm test`
- `node tests/test-issue-467-terminal-watch.mjs`
- `node tests/test-issue-1686-log-command.mjs`
- `node tests/test-telegram-bot-dry-run.mjs`
- `node tests/test-isolation-runner.mjs`
- `node tests/docs-validation.mjs`
- `node tests/test-docs-options-sync.mjs`
- Local `.mjs` line-limit check: no files over 1500 lines
title: Add Telegram terminal watch command
